title: silene vulgaris subsp. aetnensis
intro: Silene vulgaris subsp. aetnensis, also known as the Sicilian catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that have been used to treat various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant properties, and it has also been used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Silene vulgaris subsp. aetnensis include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal benefits.
